name: Mobile Phase 4 — Home Tunnel (Cloudflare)
outcome_goal: The Mac's api-server is reachable from cellular via a persistent Cloudflare Tunnel managed by launchd. A caffeinate policy holds the Mac awake while any mobile session, SSE client, or running worker is active, releasing after 10 min idle. Daily heartbeat probes the tunnel; drop >15 min while Mac is awake fires a policy_drift event. A praxis CLI 'deploy tunnel' subcommand validates and records deployment events. Integration smoke test exercises /mobile/status through a localhost fake cloudflared.
anti_requirements: Do NOT require a custom domain — Cloudflare Tunnel's CF-managed TLS hostname is the target.
Do NOT run cloudflared under sudo or as root — user launchd agent only.
Do NOT keep the Mac awake with an always-on caffeinate — only hold when sessions/SSE/workers are active.
Do NOT widen writes outside scripts/, runtime/ supervisor + caffeinate + daily_heartbeat, cli/commands/, and one migration.
Do NOT expose any endpoint beyond /mobile/status and /auth/* publicly until Phase 5 PWA lands.
notes: Greenfield home-origin build. Replaces the retired VPS deploy plan. Persistence via caffeinate -dimsu; Mac may sleep when idle — Phase 8 Telegram is the wake-channel fallback. One-time interactive 'cloudflared login' step is documented in install_cloudflared.sh; plist can't run until login completes.

jobs.label: wave0_cloudflared_launchd
jobs.description: launchd agent running cloudflared as a persistent user service + bootstrap script for first-time login.
jobs.outcome_goal: scripts/com.praxis.cloudflared.plist installs as a user LaunchAgent that runs `cloudflared tunnel --config ~/.cloudflared/praxis.yml run` with RunAtLoad + KeepAlive. scripts/install_cloudflared.sh walks Nate through `cloudflared login` and writes ~/.cloudflared/praxis.yml pointing at api-server :8420.
jobs.prompt:
Files to create (absolute paths relative to repo root):

1. scripts/com.praxis.cloudflared.plist — launchd LaunchAgent plist with:
   - Label: com.praxis.cloudflared
   - ProgramArguments: ['/opt/homebrew/bin/cloudflared', 'tunnel', '--config', '$HOME/.cloudflared/praxis.yml', 'run']
   - RunAtLoad: true
   - KeepAlive: true (so the tunnel restarts on crash)
   - StandardOutPath: /tmp/com.praxis.cloudflared.stdout.log
   - StandardErrorPath: /tmp/com.praxis.cloudflared.stderr.log
   - EnvironmentVariables: PATH=/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin

2. scripts/install_cloudflared.sh — bash script (set -euo pipefail) that:
   - Verifies brew install cloudflared is present; if not, installs it.
   - Runs `cloudflared tunnel login` (INTERACTIVE — prints 'open this URL in your browser').
   - Prompts for a tunnel name (default 'praxis'); runs `cloudflared tunnel create <name>`; captures the generated tunnel UUID.
   - Writes ~/.cloudflared/praxis.yml with:
       tunnel: <UUID>
       credentials-file: $HOME/.cloudflared/<UUID>.json
       ingress:
         - hostname: praxis.<cf-subdomain>.trycloudflare.com   # or user-provided
           service: http://localhost:8420
         - service: http_status:404
   - Copies the plist into ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ and runs `launchctl load -w ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.praxis.cloudflared.plist`.
   - Prints the final tunnel hostname + a reminder to set MOBILE_RP_ID and MOBILE_ORIGIN in .env.

3. ~/.cloudflared/praxis.yml.example (tracked in repo at scripts/praxis.yml.example) — the ingress block above with placeholder UUID/hostname.

Verification (manual + scripted):
- test -f scripts/com.praxis.cloudflared.plist
- plutil -lint scripts/com.praxis.cloudflared.plist returns 'OK'
- test -x scripts/install_cloudflared.sh
- shellcheck scripts/install_cloudflared.sh returns zero
- bash -n scripts/install_cloudflared.sh parses

Do not run the script as part of verification — it is interactive.
jobs.verify_command: test -f scripts/com.praxis.cloudflared.plist && plutil -lint scripts/com.praxis.cloudflared.plist && test -x scripts/install_cloudflared.sh && bash -n scripts/install_cloudflared.sh

jobs.label: wave0_tunnel_env_wire
jobs.description: Extend .env.example with tunnel variables and assert MOBILE_RP_ID matches MOBILE_ORIGIN in WebAuthn config.
jobs.outcome_goal: runtime/webauthn/config.py reads MOBILE_RP_ID and MOBILE_ORIGIN from env; asserts origin ends with '://' + rp_id at startup. .env.example gains the three new vars with comments.
jobs.prompt:
1. Update .env.example — append a section:
   # Cloudflare Tunnel (Phase 4). Fill after running scripts/install_cloudflared.sh.
   CF_TUNNEL_ID=
   MOBILE_RP_ID=praxis.<cf-subdomain>.trycloudflare.com
   MOBILE_ORIGIN=https://praxis.<cf-subdomain>.trycloudflare.com

2. Add Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/webauthn/config.py with:
   - @dataclass(frozen=True) class WebAuthnConfig(rp_id: str, rp_name: str, origin: str).
   - load_webauthn_config() reads from env. Defaults rp_id='localhost', origin='http://localhost:8420' for dev. Raises ValueError if MOBILE_ORIGIN does not end with '://' + MOBILE_RP_ID when both set.

3. Wire load_webauthn_config() into runtime/webauthn/ceremonies.py — replace any hard-coded rp_id references with the config-driven value.

4. Unit tests at tests/unit/test_webauthn_config.py: dev defaults; valid tunnel config; mismatch RP_ID vs ORIGIN raises.

jobs.label: wave1_caffeinate_persistence
jobs.description: Extend praxis_supervisor.py with a CaffeinatePolicy that holds caffeinate -dimsu while any mobile_sessions / SSE / worker is active; releases after 10 min idle.
jobs.outcome_goal: runtime.caffeinate_policy.CaffeinatePolicy.is_held() returns True when any reason is active. The supervisor spawns `caffeinate -dimsu <pid>` as a child process while held, SIGTERMs it when all reasons go idle for 10 min. /mobile/status mac_awake reflects CaffeinatePolicy.is_held().
jobs.prompt:
Files to create:
- Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/caffeinate_policy.py with:
    - @dataclass(frozen=True) class CaffeinateReason(kind: Literal['session','sse','worker'], identifier: str, acquired_at: datetime).
    - class CaffeinatePolicy:
        __init__(idle_release_seconds=600)
        acquire(kind, identifier) — adds reason; if process not running, spawns `caffeinate -dimsu <current_pid>`.
        release(kind, identifier) — removes reason; if no reasons for idle_release_seconds, SIGTERMs the caffeinate child.
        is_held() -> bool — True if any reason active OR within idle window.
        tick(now=None) — called periodically by the supervisor; checks idle window and releases if elapsed.
        active_reasons() -> list[dict] — for /mobile/status.
    - Thread-safe (threading.Lock around the reasons set).
    - Module-level singleton _policy = CaffeinatePolicy(); accessed via get_caffeinate_policy().

Wire-in points:
1. runtime/praxis_supervisor.py — on startup, import caffeinate_policy and call tick() on its existing periodic loop (every 30s). On shutdown, release all reasons + SIGTERM caffeinate child.
2. surfaces/api/_mobile_session.py — on successful session validation, call caffeinate_policy.acquire('session', session_id). On session revocation, release.
3. surfaces/api/handlers/approvals.py SSE endpoint — on client connect, acquire('sse', request_id). On disconnect, release.
4. runtime/workers/ (grep the worker loop entrypoint) — on worker start, acquire('worker', worker_id). On worker exit, release.
5. surfaces/api/handlers/mobile_status.py — replace the stub mac_awake=True with caffeinate_policy.is_held().

Tests at tests/integration/test_caffeinate_policy.py using a fake subprocess runner (monkeypatch subprocess.Popen so no real caffeinate runs): acquire spawns; double-acquire single spawn; release drops one ref; last release + idle elapsed triggers SIGTERM; is_held() reflects state; thread-safety under concurrent acquire/release.

jobs.label: wave1_tunnel_health_probe
jobs.description: Daily heartbeat probes the tunnel; drop >15 min while Mac is awake emits policy_drift.
jobs.outcome_goal: Running runtime.daily_heartbeat with scope='tunnel' resolves the CF hostname, curls /mobile/status, records a heartbeat_probe_snapshots row with probe_kind='tunnel'. Missed >15 min while caffeinate_policy.is_held() inserts a policy_drift_events row.
jobs.prompt:
1. Migration Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/192_heartbeat_scope_constraint_tunnel.sql widens the existing heartbeat_probe_snapshots probe_kind + scope CHECK constraints to admit 'tunnel'. Pattern mirrors migration 192_heartbeat_scope_model_retirement.sql (if it exists; else 191). Register in workflow_migration_authority.json.

2. Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/daily_heartbeat.py — add a new probe function probe_tunnel(conn):
   - Reads MOBILE_ORIGIN from env. If unset, skip with probe_kind='tunnel', status='skipped', note='MOBILE_ORIGIN unset'.
   - aiohttp GET f'{MOBILE_ORIGIN}/mobile/status' with 10s timeout.
   - On 200 with {ok: true}: insert heartbeat_probe_snapshots row, status='green'.
   - On timeout/error: status='red'; check caffeinate_policy.is_held(); if held and >15 min since last green row for probe_kind='tunnel', call runtime.capability.policy_drift.emit_drift(policy_id='infra::home-tunnel', decision_ref='decision.2026-04-21.home-origin-cloudflare-tunnel', detector_kind='tunnel_unreachable_while_mac_awake', violator_path=MOBILE_ORIGIN, action_summary=f'Tunnel unreachable {duration}m while caffeinate held').
   - Register probe_tunnel in the daily_heartbeat scope dispatch so `daily_heartbeat --scope tunnel` and `daily_heartbeat --scope all` both invoke it.

3. Tests at Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/integration/test_tunnel_heartbeat.py using monkeypatched aiohttp: 200 response writes green row; timeout writes red + no drift if Mac not held; timeout + held + >15 min since last green -> drift row written.

jobs.label: wave2_deploy_workflow_cli
jobs.description: praxis workflow deploy tunnel CLI — validates cloudflared is running, records a deployment_events row.
jobs.outcome_goal: praxis workflow deploy tunnel runs, checks `launchctl list com.praxis.cloudflared` returns 0, curls /mobile/status through the tunnel, inserts a deployment_events row. Nonzero exit on any failure with remediation hints.
jobs.prompt:
1. Migration Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/197_deployment_events.sql:
   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS deployment_events (
       deployment_id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
       target        text NOT NULL,  -- e.g. 'tunnel'
       status        text NOT NULL CHECK (status IN ('success','failure','in_progress')),
       started_at    timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
       finished_at   timestamptz NULL,
       actor         text,
       notes         text,
       evidence      jsonb
   );
   CREATE INDEX idx_deployment_events_target_started ON deployment_events (target, started_at DESC);
   Register in authority.

2. Code&DBs/Workflow/cli/commands/deploy.py:
   - Register subcommand 'deploy' under `praxis workflow` with arg 'target' (positional; first target: 'tunnel').
   - For target='tunnel':
     a. Run `launchctl list com.praxis.cloudflared`; expect exit 0.
     b. Read MOBILE_ORIGIN from env; curl ${MOBILE_ORIGIN}/mobile/status with 10s timeout; expect 200 + ok=true.
     c. INSERT deployment_events row with status 'success' or 'failure' + evidence jsonb containing the two step results.
   - On failure, print remediation hint: 'Run scripts/install_cloudflared.sh if not yet installed; or tail /tmp/com.praxis.cloudflared.stderr.log'.

3. Tests at Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/integration/test_deploy_tunnel_cli.py: mocked subprocess + aiohttp; success writes a row with status='success'; launchctl failure writes status='failure' + evidence captures the stderr.

jobs.label: wave3_tunnel_smoke_test
jobs.description: Integration test: localhost fake cloudflared exercises /mobile/status round-trip.
jobs.outcome_goal: tests/integration/test_tunnel_smoke.py spins up a local HTTP proxy acting as cloudflared, curls api-server :8420/mobile/status through it, asserts 200 + the four documented keys. Failure modes are named in comments.
jobs.prompt:
Add Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/integration/test_tunnel_smoke.py:

- Fixture: start a minimal aiohttp reverse proxy on a random port that forwards localhost:8420 -> fake CF endpoint; this is the 'fake cloudflared'.
- Fixture: start the api-server in-process (or assume running on :8420 — prefer in-process to avoid dep on launchctl).
- Test: GET {fake_cf_endpoint}/mobile/status -> 200 with ok, server_ts, last_approval_age_s, mac_awake keys.
- Test: shut down the api-server; GET returns 502 from the proxy.
- Document in module docstring:
    Real-world failure modes this test proxies for:
      - cloudflared not running -> 502 at the CF edge (proxy simulates this when backend is down).
      - api-server down -> 502 through the tunnel (same simulation).
      - DNS lag on first deploy -> NXDOMAIN (not simulated — live-test only).
      - Mac asleep -> tunnel disconnects from CF edge (not simulated — rely on Phase 8 Telegram wake).

Verification: `pytest test_tunnel_smoke.py` green.
